trim_reasons: PHI is a +11.5 underdog, creating genuine garbage-time risk in Q4 where Maxey's minutes may compress if BOS runs away early. Despite the blowout penalty already applied, the structural risk of a double-digit deficit leading to early benching of key players on a fading team remains — and PHI's team_momentum is neutral, not hot, suggesting limited capacity to keep the game close.

Tatum is a volatile player on the favored side of an 11.5-point blowout game, and AST picks on volatile scorers in blowout-risk games face compounded suppression: BOS may pull starters in Q4, eliminating late assist opportunities that would otherwise push the total over. The historical lesson that AST picks carry systematic overconfidence risk — especially when opp_today is not soft and game script could resolve early — applies here even at the conservative T6 tier.

Brown is flagged volatile with a min_floor of 29.9 and BLOWOUT_RISK on the favored side at -11.5 — in a game BOS controls early, starters routinely see Q4 rest, which is precisely where a volatile scorer's floor becomes fragile. The FG_MARGIN_NEG cascade already stepped down to T20, but Brown's wide distribution means a single cold-shooting quarter combined with early exit still produces a miss at this threshold.

Pritchard's min_floor of 23.4 is the primary structural vulnerability: as a bench player on the BLOWOUT_RISK side, a game that gets away from PHI early could see Pritchard's minutes compressed well below his average, and 23.4 as the floor leaves meaningful exposure to a sub-10-point outcome in a reduced-minutes scenario. Bench rotational players are most susceptible to early garbage-time compression.

White is flagged as a LATE_FADER, and the blowout-risk game script on the favored side is the exact scenario where late-game fading is most consequential — BOS protecting a lead in Q4 reduces pace and possession volume, directly compressing assist opportunities for a role player whose floor depends on run-the-offense minutes. The series AST record at T4 was already 1/4=25%, requiring a skip to T2, suggesting AST production has been genuinely inconsistent in this series.

Brunson is flagged volatile with FG_MARGIN_THIN at 0% — essentially no FG safety margin — meaning his hit rate is heavily dependent on efficient shooting nights, and a single cold-FG game produces a miss even at this conservative T15 tier. The volatile tag combined with near-zero FG margin creates a structurally fragile floor that the tier step-down partially but not fully mitigates.

Anunoby's raw_avg_REB of 5.8 sits below the T6 threshold, meaning he needs an above-average rebounding game just to clear the line — and his volatility flag (volatile) combined with a raw_avg below the target creates genuine negative-margin exposure. The quant tier being listed at T4 overall=75% while this pick targets T6 suggests the structural floor for this REB tier is thinner than the series/H2H data alone implies.

Wembanyama's min_floor of 15.6 against an avg of 27.9 reveals extreme minutes volatility — a single game where he plays 20 minutes (well within historical variance for this player) produces a near-certain miss at PTS T15. This is not an elimination game, so the minutes fragility exception does not apply, and a 15.6 min_floor on a PTS T15 pick represents exactly the structural fragility the rules flag as disqualifying for high-confidence picks.

Fox faces a tough defense on the favored side of an 11.5-point blowout game — the historical lesson explicitly identifies that tough defenses generating turnovers cannibalize assist opportunities for primary ball-handlers, and the spread being >5 points in a game SAS is expected to control creates late-game pace reduction that further suppresses AST accumulation. The series hit rate at T6 (3/4=75%) is adequate but not dominant.

Harper's min_floor of 19.8 against avg 25.1 is structurally marginal for an AST prop — a game where he plays 20–22 minutes (within normal variance) leaves very little cushion to accumulate 3+ assists, and his post_miss bounce-back lift is negative (0.81), meaning recent misses do not reliably precede recoveries. The series AST record at T4 was 0/4=0%, requiring a full skip to T2, which underscores that this player's AST production in this series has been genuinely fragile.
